description_long: This object is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a large, single-edged steel blade. The handle is made from a natural wood material, showing a textured grain and a gentle ergonomic curve for improved grip and control. The blade is broad and wedge-shaped, with a sharp cutting edge and a flat poll opposite the edge, suitable for splitting or chopping. The transition between the handle and blade is seamless, with the blade mounted securely onto the handle in a traditional style. The axe shows no visible branding or decorative markings, emphasizing a utilitarian design. Its size and construction suggest it is intended for manual use in tasks such as chopping wood or clearing brush in urban or suburban environments.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, sharp steel blade, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The design is utilitarian, with no visible markings or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped steel blade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the sharp edge of the blade and the curvature of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the flat poll of the blade and the handle's wood grain.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the blade and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a sharp cutting edge and flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
